definitions.clavicular head of pectoralis major musc: A muscle head forming the superior portion of the pectoralis major, arising from the anterior surface of the medial half of the clavicle, whose fibers pass obliquely downward and laterally to insert onto the lateral lip of the bicipital groove of the humerus, and acting primarily to flex and medially rotate the arm at the shoulder joint, innervated by lateral pectoral nerve (C5–C6).
